title: Architecture and Pipeline
text: ksAI offline reasoning layer top ksTFL clinical outputs. ksTFL computes tables, figures, listings deterministically writes meta folder JSON (structural specification plus rendered data). ksAI reads folder, compiles outputs self-contained context objects, routes language model two complementary pipelines: Direct context pipeline — already know outputs matter fit model window. Clinical capsule pipeline — study large, exploratory, token-constrained; decompose, enrich, retrieve, reason minimal evidence set. pipelines share import layer CKR (Clinical Knowledge Representation) serialization formats. differ much structure built reasoning model runs.

title: Architecture and Pipeline
text: ksTFL’s save_report() writes meta folder: spec JSON may contain one outputs. ksAI resolves stable output ids (example 14-3.01, 14-5.01) document filenames spec keys. ks_list_ids(meta_path) scans index returns id, type, title without loading data. ks_load(meta_path, ids = ...) parses requested outputs assembles ks_study.

title: Architecture and Pipeline
text: ks_context central value object. thin metadata extract; full render-join specification data: Row metadata lifted invisible control columns import: section — example Baseline, Week 24, Change Baseline kind — AE tables: SOC, PT, OVERALL; continuous endpoints: PARAM metadata drives CSR writing (“describe Week 24 vs Baseline”) capsule hierarchy inference. Truncation intentional: protects model context windows. Use as_facts() / capsules structured access specific rows without sending full table.
code: ks_context ├── id, type, title, subtitles ├── population, source ├── columns[]          # visible cols: name, label, type, is_grouping ├── span_headers[]     # treatment-arm groups ├── rows[]             # cells + section + kind (ROW_KIND, SECTION, …) ├── n_rows_total       # before max_rows truncation ├── footnotes[] └── annotations{}      # user overlays via enrich_context()

title: Architecture and Pipeline
text: CKR token-efficient, retrieval-capable representation stack ks_context. Three formats, increasing structure: Default reading models benefit familiar table layout. Repeats column headers arm labels per row group. Highest token cost. Pure R. Target: ~50–70% fewer tokens markdown span-heavy tables. Span tables emit one-time legend plus short keys per row: Non-span tables use column header line plus pipe-delimited rows. Controlled ks_set_option(context_format = "compact") ks_llm() ks_chat(). C++23 FactTable + InvertedIndex (Rcpp XPtr). One table becomes columnar store: retrieve(facts, rows = ..., sections = ..., spans = ...) subsets rows optionally filters treatment-arm span columns, as_compact.ks_facts() renders matched slice. use: know structural filter (one SOC, one visit, one parameter row) want zero LLM tokens spent irrelevant rows.
code: TABLE: 14-5.01 | Population: Safety TITLE: ... SPANS: A=Placebo (N=86); B=Xanomeline Low (N=84); C=Xanomeline High (N=84) COLS: n(%), [AEs] [CARDIAC DISORDERS] SINUS BRADYCARDIA:  A: 2 (2.3%), [2]  |  B: 7 (8.3%), [10]  |  C: 8 (9.5%), [12] facts_ae <- as_facts(study[["14-5.01"]]) retrieve(facts_ae, rows = "CARDIAC DISORDERS") |> as_compact()

title: Architecture and Pipeline
text: large studies exploratory questions, ksAI decomposes outputs clinical capsules — concept-centric, traceable semantic units — runs four-stage agent pipeline.     Splits ks_context hierarchical row groups builds ks_capsule_store. Domain inferred per output (per capsule). Domain inference (language-agnostic; first hit wins): enrich_context(..., annotations = list(domain = ...)) Session domain_map (ks_set_option()) — exact id regex MedDRA-like structure (ROW_KIND SOC/PT/…) → AE Optional small LLM model set llm_domain = "always" Multilingual title/subtitle/source lexicon ICH/CSR-style numbering output id (14-5.x → AE, …) Optional small LLM still UNKNOWN (llm_domain = "unknown", default) "UNKNOWN" Capsule anatomy: Hierarchy inference uses ROW_KIND, SECTION, row labels: Synthetic SOC capsules created section blocks source table uses section headers without explicit ROW_KIND = SOC. Persist save_capsules(store, "study.ksc") / load_capsules(). Two-pass enrichment, plus optional domain repair: Pure R pass (always): tokenize label + compact_text, strip stop words, detect clinical abbreviations (TEAE, SOC, PT, LOCF, …). Optional small-LLM pass: structured JSON extraction concepts, synonyms, keywords. 4B local model sufficient. Domain fallback (model set): reclassify capsules still tagged UNKNOWN, per source_id. Use force_domain = TRUE reclassify every table. Run per study; reuse .ksc file across writing sessions. ks_embed() calls OpenAI-compatible /v1/embeddings endpoint stores vectors capsule$embedding. ks_retrieve(store, query, n, filter, weights) scores every capsule: Returns ks_capsule_subset ranked capsules score breakdown. Filters narrow candidates scoring: embeddings absent, semantic score falls back 0 keyword/metadata signals still rank results. Combines retrieval generation: ks_retrieve() → top n capsules Optionally expand = TRUE → include child PT capsules (progressive disclosure) Format capsule compact_text + metadata context block Send larger reasoning model focused system prompt reasoning model never sees full study — retrieved capsule summaries. capsule pipeline’s primary token advantage. High-level reasoning happens SOC summaries; detail rows fetched query expand flag requires .
code: flowchart LR   subgraph structure["Structure agent"]     AC["as_capsules()"]   end   subgraph semantic["Semantic agent"]     AN["ks_annotate()"]   end   subgraph retrieval["Retrieval agent"]     EM["ks_embed()"]     RT["ks_retrieve()"]   end   subgraph reasoning["Reasoning agent"]     RS["ks_reason()"]   end    CTX["ks_context"] --> AC --> STORE["ks_capsule_store"]   STORE --> AN --> STORE   STORE --> EM --> STORE   STORE --> RT --> SUB["ks_capsule_subset"]   SUB --> RS --> OUT["ks_result"] store <- as_capsules(study)   # rules only  store <- as_capsules(   study,   model = "qwen3.5-4b",   provider = "lm_studio",   base_url = "http://127.0.0.1:1234",   llm_domain = "unknown",   llm_min_confidence = 0.5 ) ks_capsule ├── capsule_id          # e.g. 14-5.01::SOC::CARDIAC_DISORDERS ├── source_id           # output id ├── source_rows         # traceable row indices ├── domain              # AE, EFFC, DM, LB, VS, EX, DS, … (rules + optional small LLM) ├── level               # OVERALL → SOC → PT  (or OVERALL → PARAM) ├── label, population ├── parent_id, child_ids ├── linked_ids          # text listings in same domain ├── stats               # parsed n(%), means per arm ├── compact_text        # token-efficient summary for the LLM ├── concepts, keywords, synonyms └── embedding           # numeric vector (after ks_embed) store <- ks_annotate(store) store <- ks_annotate(   store,   model = "qwen3.5-4b",   provider = "lm_studio",   base_url = "http://127.0.0.1:1234",   force_domain = FALSE,   llm_min_confidence = 0.5 ) ks_retrieve(store,   query = "cardiac bradycardia high dose",   n = 5,   filter = list(domain = "AE", level = "SOC")) ks_reason(store,   query = "Summarize cardiac findings with n(%) per arm",   n = 5,   expand = TRUE,   model = "gemma-4-26b-a4b-it-mlx",   provider = "lm_studio") Query → retrieve SOC capsules (compact summaries)       → reason at SOC level       → expand = TRUE pulls PT children on demand       → linked_ids connect to patient listings (same domain)

title: Architecture and Pipeline
text: Deterministic upstream — ksTFL numbers final; ksAI never recomputes statistics. Targeted loading — load ids need; scale full studies via capsules. Traceability — every capsule fact links back source_id + source_rows. Token economy — compact DSL, fact filtering, capsule retrieval minimize model input. Offline-first — JSON-import; local models via Ollama LM Studio. Separation concerns — structure (R), retrieval (C++23), semantics (small LLM), reasoning (large LLM). layers compose: can use as_compact() without capsules, capsules without embeddings (keyword-retrieval), full stack large-study Q&.

what: Why a capsule pipeline (instead of dumping full tables)
title: Clinical Capsule Pipeline — Extended Pilot Study Example
text: Medical writers rarely need every row every table one question. Full injection large AE laboratory table 8k-token local model fails forces aggressive max_rows truncation silently drops evidence. capsule pipeline solves changing reasoning model sees: clinical capsule concept-centric unit (example one SOC, one PT, one demographic parameter) : traceability — source_id + source_rows point back table; compact evidence — compact_text parsed stats LLM; hierarchy — parent_id / child_ids progressive disclosure; retrieval handles — domain, keywords, optional embedding.     Build store per study (per writing campaign). Reuse .ksc file across sessions without reloading ksTFL JSON.

title: Clinical Capsule Pipeline — Extended Pilot Study Example
text: ksAI ships self-contained meta folder CDISC Pilot Study (ksTFL save_report() artefacts): Resolve runtime : development checkout install, use: walkthrough written LM Studio three roles: URL rule matters: Chat / annotate / reason: http://127.0.0.1:1234 (/v1) Embeddings: http://127.0.0.1:1234/v1 (/v1) ellmer’s chat_lmstudio() appends /v1 . Passing /v1 chat constructors makes model discovery fail even model loaded.
code: inst/examples/pilot-study/meta/   _index.json   *.json          # specs + data   *.svg           # figures (ignored by capsule build for tables) meta_path <- system.file(   "examples", "pilot-study", "meta",   package = "ksAI" ) dir.exists(meta_path) meta_path <- file.path("inst", "examples", "pilot-study", "meta") library(ksAI)  # Capsule demos keep tables modest so compact_text stays readable. # The full pilot AE table can exceed 200 capsules at max_rows = 200; # 20–50 is enough to demonstrate hierarchy and retrieval. ks_set_option(   max_rows = 40L,   context_format = "compact",   provider = "lm_studio",   embed_model = "text-embedding-nomic-embed-text-v1.5",   embed_url = "http://127.0.0.1:1234/v1" )  RUN_LLM <- FALSE   # TRUE when LM Studio is up with the models above CHAT_URL <- "http://127.0.0.1:1234" MODEL_SMALL <- "qwen3.5-4b" MODEL_LARGE <- "qwen3.6-14b-a3b-fablevibes" #MODEL_LARGE <- "gemma-4-14b-a4b-heretic-v2"  OUT_DIR <- file.path(tempdir(), "ksAI-capsule-pilot") dir.create(OUT_DIR, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

what: Step 0 — Choose outputs that span clinical domains
title: Clinical Capsule Pipeline — Extended Pilot Study Example
text: Domain tags capsules inferred .capsule_infer_domain() language-agnostic order: Explicit annotations$domain via enrich_context() Session domain_map (ks_set_option()) — exact output id regex Structural cues (ROW_KIND SOC/PT/HLGT/… → AE) Optional small LLM as_capsules(..., model = ..., llm_domain = "always") Multilingual title/subtitle/source lexicon (EN, DE, FR, ES, , RU, ZH, JA, …) ICH/CSR-style numbering output id (14-5.x → AE, 14-6.x → LB, …) Optional small LLM still UNKNOWN (model + default llm_domain = "unknown") "UNKNOWN" Bare English "baseline" alone mapped DM (avoids tagging efficacy change--baseline tables demographics). Prefer enrich_context(..., annotations = list(domain = ...)), domain_map, small local model study-specific / non-English titles: deliberately load six tables across five domains retrieval filters domain inventory non-trivial:
code: store <- as_capsules(   study,   model = "qwen3.5-4b",   provider = "lm_studio",   base_url = "http://127.0.0.1:1234",   llm_domain = "unknown"   # or "always" after hard signals ) # Later: re-tag remaining UNKNOWN domains during annotate store <- ks_annotate(store, model = "qwen3.5-4b", provider = "lm_studio",                      base_url = "http://127.0.0.1:1234") meta_path <- system.file("examples", "pilot-study", "meta", package = "ksAI")  TABLES <- c(   demographics   = "14-2.01",  # DM  — Age, Sex, Race, …   efficacy_adas  = "14-3.01",  # EFFC   efficacy_cibic = "14-3.02",  # EFFC   adverse_events = "14-5.01",  # AE   — SOC / PT hierarchy   laboratory     = "14-6.01",  # LB   — visit / parameter rows   vital_signs    = "14-7.02"   # VS   — change from baseline )  # Discover without loading data JSON: catalog <- ks_list_ids(meta_path) catalog[catalog$id %in% TABLES, c("id", "type", "title")]

title: Clinical Capsule Pipeline — Extended Pilot Study Example
text: as_capsules(study) walks every loaded context : Infers domain per output (rules; optional model / llm_domain / llm_min_confidence small LLM available). Identifies row-label column (first visible column). AE-style: OVERALL → SOC → PT (ROW_KIND / section headers); continuous / demo / lab: OVERALL → PARAM. Builds synthetic SOC capsules section header exists without explicit SOC kind row. Parses arm-wise stats possible. Renders compact_text row slice . Wires parent_id / child_ids. Links linked_ids text listings inferred domain (text outputs loaded). Always print inventory annotation. surfaces domain mis-tags unexpected capsule counts early. Pick one AE SOC capsule one demographic PARAM capsule: “good” looks like AE SOC capsule: level == "SOC", domain == "AE"; child_ids lists PT capsule ids SOC; compact_text contains arm-keyed incidence lines, whole AE table; source_rows contiguous section-scoped index set audit study[["14-5.01"]]$rows. Compare demographics capsule: PARAM capsules flatter: fewer children, one characteristic visit row, still arm-spanned compact text source table uses span headers. Even enrichment, save store can resume without rebuilding:

title: Clinical Capsule Pipeline — Extended Pilot Study Example
text: ks_annotate(store) without model argument always runs pure-R pass: tokenizes label + compact_text (+ existing concepts); drops small stopword list; maps known abbreviations synonyms (teae → “treatment emergent adverse event”, soc → “system organ class”, …); seeds concepts label empty. pass matters: keyword overlap 30% default ks_retrieve() score. capsule empty keywords embedding ranks metadata filters. Always run least R pass retrieval demos. 4B local model can add clinical concepts synonyms pure tokenization misses (example mapping “SINUS BRADYCARDIA” broader cardiac terms). model argument also reclassifies capsules still tagged UNKNOWN (per source_id). Use force_domain = TRUE reclassify every table. slower memory-heavy: unload 26B model LM Studio first needed. LLM instructed return JSON keys concepts, synonyms, keywords. Non-JSON wrappers stripped possible; failed parses leave capsule unchanged field.
code: store <- ks_annotate(store)  # Keywords are now retrieval fodder even without embeddings cap_soc <- store$capsules[[cardiac_ids[[1]]]] head(cap_soc$keywords, 30) cap_soc$synonyms cap_soc$concepts if (RUN_LLM) {   # Annotate a representative subset first — full stores can be large.   demo_ids <- unique(c(     cardiac_ids[1],     age_ids[1],     grep("14-3.01", names(store$capsules), value = TRUE)[1],     grep("14-6.01", names(store$capsules), value = TRUE)[1],     grep("14-7.02", names(store$capsules), value = TRUE)[1]   ))    demo <- store   demo$capsules <- store$capsules[demo_ids]    demo <- ks_annotate(     demo,     model              = MODEL_SMALL,     provider           = "lm_studio",     base_url           = CHAT_URL,     force              = TRUE,   # re-run even if concepts already seeded     force_domain       = FALSE,  # only repair UNKNOWN domains     llm_min_confidence = 0.5   )    store$capsules[demo_ids] <- demo$capsules    for (cid in demo_ids) {     cat("\n== ", cid, " ==\n", sep = "")     print(store$capsules[[cid]]$concepts)     print(store$capsules[[cid]]$synonyms)   } }

title: Clinical Capsule Pipeline — Extended Pilot Study Example
text: capsule, ks_retrieve() computes: score=ws⋅semantic+wk⋅keyword+wm⋅metadata \text{score} = w_s \cdot \text{semantic} + w_k \cdot \text{keyword} + w_m \cdot \text{metadata} Defaults: ws=0.6w_s = 0.6, wk=0.3w_k = 0.3, wm=0.1w_m = 0.1. Filters (domain, level, population) hard-exclude capsules current implementation; contribute metadata score. Prefer post-filtering subset inspecting scores need hard domain gate. read scores: High semantic, low keyword — paraphrased clinical language matched embedding space. High keyword, low semantic — shared tokens (embeddings). High metadata — filter fields matched; still compare raw rank. Inspect winning capsule’s evidence reasoning:
code: queries <- list(   ae_cardiac = list(     query  = "cardiac disorders and bradycardia in the high dose arm",     filter = list(domain = "AE", level = "SOC")   ),   efficacy = list(     query  = "ADAS-Cog change from baseline week 24 treatment difference",     filter = list(level = "PARAM")   ),   laboratory = list(     query  = "laboratory summary statistics change from baseline over visits",     filter = list(domain = "LB")   ),   vital_signs = list(     query  = "systolic blood pressure change from baseline end of treatment",     filter = list(level = "PARAM")   ),   demographics = list(     query  = "age sex race baseline demographic characteristics",     filter = list(domain = "DM", level = "PARAM")   ) )  if (RUN_LLM) {   subsets <- lapply(names(queries), function(nm) {     q <- queries[[nm]]     cat("\n######## ", nm, " ########\n", sep = "")     sub <- ks_retrieve(       store,       query  = q$query,       n      = 5L,       filter = q$filter     )     print(sub)     print(sub$scores)     sub   })   names(subsets) <- names(queries) } else {   # Offline smoke: keyword-only retrieval still ranks something useful   sub_offline <- ks_retrieve(     store,     query = "cardiac bradycardia",     n = 5L,     filter = list(domain = "AE"),     # Without embeddings, semantic weight is wasted — shift mass to keywords:     weights = list(semantic = 0, keyword = 0.8, metadata = 0.2)   )   print(sub_offline)   print(sub_offline$scores) } # Using the offline or first live subset: top_id <- names(sub_offline$capsules)[[1]] cat(store$capsules[[top_id]]$compact_text)

text: ks_reason() retrieve + generate one call: ks_retrieve(store, query, n) (scoring ); optional progressive disclosure — expand = TRUE, pull every child_ids capsule store prompt; format capsule metadata + compact_text context block; chat larger model system prompt forbids inventing statistics. Use expand = TRUE question needs preferred-term detail system organ class. Use FALSE SOC-level (PARAM-level) summaries already answer question — smaller prompts, less noise, lower chance context overflow local 8k models. model instructed invent numbers, must: Check cited n(%) values appear printed capsule compact_text original table; Remember max_rows truncated source table — rare PTs row cap never became capsules; Treat domain tags retrieval aids, regulatory classifications.
code: if (RUN_LLM) {   # 6a. Safety narrative at SOC level, then expand PT children   out_ae <- ks_reason(     store,     query    = "Summarize cardiac disorder incidence across treatment arms. Cite specific n(%) values from the capsules.",     n        = 6L,     expand   = TRUE,     model    = MODEL_LARGE,     provider = "lm_studio",     base_url = CHAT_URL   )   out_ae   cat(out_ae$response)    # 6b. Efficacy question — usually PARAM capsules, expand often unnecessary   out_eff <- ks_reason(     store,     query    = "What treatment differences for ADAS-Cog change from baseline at Week 24 appear in the capsules?",     n        = 6L,     expand   = FALSE,     model    = MODEL_LARGE,     provider = "lm_studio",     base_url = CHAT_URL   )   cat(out_eff$response)    # 6c. Cross-domain question — do not over-filter; let retrieval pick   out_mix <- ks_reason(     store,     query    = "Are there safety signals that should contextualize interpretation of the primary efficacy results?",     n        = 5L,     expand   = FALSE,     model    = MODEL_LARGE,     provider = "lm_studio",     base_url = CHAT_URL   )   cat(out_mix$response)    save_result(out_ae, file.path(OUT_DIR, "reason-cardiac"))   save_result(out_eff, file.path(OUT_DIR, "reason-adas")) } expand = FALSE  →  only the top-n retrieved capsules expand = TRUE   →  top-n plus all of their child_ids (e.g. SOC → PTs)

title: Targeted Workflow, Clinical Capsules, and Result Chaining
text: ksAI provides two complementary workflows reasoning ksTFL outputs. Direct context workflow (Sections 1–4): load targeted subset outputs, render markdown compact text, run skill-based prompts. fastest path single-output tasks drafting CSR narratives already know tables need. Capsule workflow (Sections 5–8): decompose outputs concept-level capsule store, enrich capsules keywords embeddings, retrieve smallest relevant subset query, send capsules larger reasoning model. preferred path exploratory questions across many outputs large studies injecting full tables exceed model context limits.

title: Targeted Workflow, Clinical Capsules, and Result Chaining
text: Alternatively create persistent chat session — useful want ask follow-questions conversational thread. Note ks_chat() embeds loaded contexts system prompt, set context_format building chat keep max_rows within model’s context window:
code: # Preferred: pass study and provider directly (context injected once). out_describe <- ks_llm(   study,   ids = "14-3.01",   skill = "describe",   model = "qwen3:14b",   provider = "ollama" )  # Multi-ID review (requires exactly two IDs). out_review <- ks_llm(   study,   ids = c("14-3.01", "14-3.02"),   skill = "review",   model = "qwen3:14b",   provider = "ollama" )  # Extra user instruction on top of a skill template. out_section <- ks_llm(   study,   ids = "14-7.02",   skill = "csr_section",   prompt = "Keep only statements supported by explicit values.",   model = "qwen3:14b",   provider = "ollama" )  # Free-form mode (no skill template). out_free <- ks_llm(   study,   ids = c("14-3.01", "14-7.02"),   skill = NULL,   prompt = "Compare efficacy and safety signals.",   model = "qwen3:14b",   provider = "ollama" ) ks_set_option(context_format = "compact", max_rows = 50L) chat <- ks_chat(study, model = "qwen3:14b", provider = "ollama") ks_llm(chat, ids = "14-3.01", skill = "describe")

title: Targeted Workflow, Clinical Capsules, and Result Chaining
text: ks_llm() ks_reason() return ks_result. Persist save_result() (writes .md .json) reload load_result(): Pass previous result prior next run can build :
code: save_result(out_section, "analysis/out-14-7-02") out_loaded <- load_result("analysis/out-14-7-02") out_refined <- ks_llm(   study,   ids = "14-7.02",   skill = "csr_section",   prompt = "Tighten the narrative; remove any unsupported claims.",   prior = out_loaded,   model = "qwen3:14b",   provider = "ollama" )

title: Targeted Workflow, Clinical Capsules, and Result Chaining
text: study many outputs individual tables large fit one prompt, decompose clinical capsules — concept-centric, hierarchical summaries parsed statistics, domain tags, links. Domain codes inferred per output (multilingual rules; optional small LLM). Hard overrides win first: enrich_context(annotations$domain) ks_set_option(domain_map = ...). hierarchy three levels default: OVERALL → SOC → PT (AE tables), OVERALL → PARAM (continuous endpoints). Parent/child IDs wired automatically. Persist store alongside .ks file:
code: # Build capsule store from the entire study (or a single context). store <- as_capsules(study) store  # Optional: resolve UNKNOWN domains with a small local model (once per table). # store <- as_capsules( #   study, #   model = "qwen3.5-4b", #   provider = "lm_studio", #   base_url = "http://127.0.0.1:1234", #   llm_domain = "unknown", #   llm_min_confidence = 0.5 # )  # Inspect individual capsules. names(store$capsules)[1:6] cap <- store$capsules[["14-5.01::SOC::CARDIAC_DISORDERS"]] cap                    # print: domain, level, label, row span, child count cap$stats              # parsed statistics per treatment arm cap$compact_text       # the text a model will see for this capsule cap$child_ids          # PT-level capsule IDs nested under this SOC cap$linked_ids         # listing output IDs in the same study / same domain save_capsules(store, "my_study.ksc") store <- load_capsules("my_study.ksc")

what: 6a. Pure-R keyword pass (always fast, no model needed)
title: Targeted Workflow, Clinical Capsules, and Result Chaining
text: tokenizes label, compact_text, existing concepts, strips stop words, detects known clinical abbreviations (TEAE, SOC, PT, etc.).
code: store <- ks_annotate(store) store$capsules[["14-5.01::SOC::CARDIAC_DISORDERS"]]$keywords

title: Targeted Workflow, Clinical Capsules, and Result Chaining
text: 4B model running locally can identify medical concepts, synonyms, richer keywords. model also reclassifies capsules still tagged UNKNOWN (per source_id). Use force_domain = TRUE reclassify every table. LM Studio qwen3.5-4b similar works well: concept response expected JSON keys concepts, synonyms, keywords. Domain responses use {"domain":"CODE","confidence":0.0}. non-JSON wrapper text stripped automatically.
code: store <- ks_annotate(   store,   model              = "qwen3.5-4b",   provider           = "lm_studio",   base_url           = "http://127.0.0.1:1234",   force              = FALSE,  # skip capsules already annotated   force_domain       = FALSE,  # only UNKNOWN domains   llm_min_confidence = 0.5 )

title: Targeted Workflow, Clinical Capsules, and Result Chaining
text: ks_embed() calls OpenAI-compatible /v1/embeddings endpoint — text-embedding-nomic-embed-text-v1.5 runs box LM Studio: Embeddings stored capsule$embedding numeric vector persisted .ksc file. ks_retrieve() falls back keyword-scoring embeddings absent.
code: ks_set_option(   embed_model = "text-embedding-nomic-embed-text-v1.5",   embed_url   = "http://127.0.0.1:1234/v1" )  store <- ks_embed(store) length(store$capsules[["14-5.01::SOC::CARDIAC_DISORDERS"]]$embedding)

title: Targeted Workflow, Clinical Capsules, and Result Chaining
text: ks_retrieve() scores every capsule query using three signals blended configurable weights:
code: subset <- ks_retrieve(   store,   query  = "cardiac events in the Xanomeline High Dose arm",   n      = 5L,   filter = list(domain = "AE", level = "SOC") )  subset                   # print: query + top-5 ranked capsules with scores names(subset$capsules)   # selected capsule IDs subset$scores            # data.frame: capsule_id, semantic, keyword, metadata, score

title: Targeted Workflow, Clinical Capsules, and Result Chaining
text: ks_reason() combines retrieval reasoning step one call. model never sees full study — capsule compact texts matched subset, plus metadata: Set expand = TRUE progressive disclosure: initial reasoning pass uses retrieved capsules; children loaded demand:
code: out <- ks_reason(   store,   query    = "Summarize cardiac findings and statistical significance",   n        = 5L,   expand   = FALSE,   # TRUE → also include child PT capsules   model    = "gemma-4-26b-a4b-it-mlx",   provider = "lm_studio" )  out                  # ks_result with skill = "reason" cat(out$response) out_expanded <- ks_reason(   store,   query    = "Which PT-level terms drove cardiac disorder incidence?",   n        = 3L,   expand   = TRUE,          # pulls in all PT children of matched SOC capsules   model    = "gemma-4-26b-a4b-it-mlx",   provider = "lm_studio" )

what: For broad tasks (many outputs or large tables)
title: Targeted Workflow, Clinical Capsules, and Result Chaining
text: ks_load(ids = NULL) → full study as_capsules(study) → concept-level decomposition (optional model / llm_domain UNKNOWN domains) ks_annotate(store) → keywords (+ optional small-LLM enrichment / force_domain domain repair) ks_embed(store) → semantic vectors via local embedding model save_capsules(store, ...) → persist reuse ks_reason(store, query, n) → retrieve + reason one call save_result() → traceable deliverable Capsule stores can built full study reused across many writing sessions without reloading reprocessing ksTFL source JSON.

title: ksAI
text: ksAI offline, AI-native reasoning layer top ksTFL clinical tables framework. reads metadata data JSON produced ksTFL::save_report(), compiles every study output self-contained, LLM-ready registry, lets medical writer reason across whole set statistical results draft Clinical Study Report (CSR) narratives — using local model via Ollama LM Studio, hosted OpenAI-compatible provider. live ksTFL session required runtime: ksAI works entirely saved JSON.

title: ksAI
text: ksAI imports ksTFL, ellmer, jsonlite, cli, checkmate, rlang.
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::local_install("path/to/ksAI")

title: ksAI
code: library(ksAI)  # 1. Discover available output IDs and load only the targets you need ks_list_ids("path/to/outputs/meta") study <- ks_load("path/to/outputs/meta", ids = c("14-3.01", "14-3.02"))  # 2. Open a chat over the loaded subset chat <- ks_chat(study, model = "qwen3:14b", provider = "ollama")  # 3. Run a skill (supports one or multiple IDs) out <- ks_llm(chat, ids = c("14-3.01", "14-3.02"), skill = "review")  # 4. Add free-form user instructions (model follows prompt language) out2 <- ks_llm(chat, ids = "14-3.01", skill = "describe", prompt = "Describe in Spanish")  # 5. Persist generated output save_result(out2, "analysis/table-14-3-01") loaded_out <- load_result("analysis/table-14-3-01")

title: ksAI
text: ks_context — render-join ksTFL specification data: title, population, columns, span headers, rendered rows (section/kind metadata), footnotes, self-contained. ks_study — registry outputs, split tables, figures, texts. Save/reload save_study() / ks_load(). ks_chat() — ellmer chat wired targeted loaded subset. ks_result — persisted output ks_llm(). Save/load save_result() / load_result(). Clinical capsules — as_capsules() decomposes large studies concept-level units language-agnostic domain tags (domain_map, multilingual lexicon, optional small-LLM via model / llm_domain). Enrich ks_annotate(), embed, retrieve, reason. Skills — customizable Markdown prompt templates. Built-ins: describe, summarize, csr_section, review. Add ks_set_option(skills_dir = ...); see ks_list_skills().

title: ksAI 0.2.0
text: as_capsules() now infers clinical domain codes priority chain works non-English titles: enrich_context(annotations$domain), session domain_map, MedDRA ROW_KIND structure, multilingual lexicon, ICH/CSR-style output ids, "UNKNOWN". New option domain_map (ks_get_option() / ks_set_option()): named character vector exact output ids regex → domain. Bare English "baseline" longer mapped DM.

title: ksAI 0.2.0
text: as_capsules(x, model = ..., provider = ..., base_url = ..., llm_domain = c("unknown","always","never"), llm_min_confidence = 0.5) can call small local model per table (chat reused across study). ks_annotate(..., force_domain = FALSE, llm_min_confidence = 0.5) model set also reclassifies remaining UNKNOWN domains (per source_id). Use force_domain = TRUE reclassify every table. Closed codes: AE, DM, VS, LB, EFFC, EX, DS, UNKNOWN (plus common aliases).
